   145→@app.post("/api/test/recommend")
   146→async def test_recommend(body: dict):
   147→    """
   148→    Test endpoint combining Serper search → Claude Haiku outfit curation →
   149→    Nano Banana flat lays → transparent overlay images.
   150→
   151→    Expects: { brands: string[], gender: string, style_notes: string }
   152→    Returns: { outfits: [{ outfit_name, voice, items: [{ id, category, imageUrl, name }] }] }
   153→    """
   154→    brands = body.get("brands", ["Nike", "Zara", "H&M"])
   155→    gender = body.get("gender", "mens")
   156→    style_notes = body.get("style_notes", "casual")
   157→
   158→    serper_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
   159→    anthropic_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
   160→
   161→    if not serper_key:
   162→        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="SERPER_API_KEY not configured")
   163→    if not anthropic_key:
   164→        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not configured")
   165→
   166→    # Step 1: Serper search for tops + bottoms
   167→    print(f"[test/recommend] Searching for {brands} ({gender}, {style_notes})")
   168→    brand_queries = build_brand_queries(brands[:5], gender)
   169→
   170→    tops_items, bottoms_items = await asyncio.gather(
   171→        fetch_clothing_batch(brand_queries["tops"], serper_key, num_results_per_query=3),
   172→        fetch_clothing_batch(brand_queries["bottoms"], serper_key, num_results_per_query=3),
   173→    )
   174→
   175→    # Limit to reasonable count before sending to Claude
   176→    tops_items = tops_items[:15]
   177→    bottoms_items = bottoms_items[:15]
   178→
   179→    print(f"[test/recommend] Found {len(tops_items)} tops, {len(bottoms_items)} bottoms")
   180→
   181→    if not tops_items and not bottoms_items:
   182→        return {"outfits": []}
   183→
   184→    # Step 2: Claude Haiku picks 2 outfits with Mira commentary
   185→    tops_list = "\n".join(
   186→        f"  T{i}: {t['title']} | {t['price']} | {t['source']} | pid:{t['product_id']} | img:{t['image_url']}"
   187→        for i, t in enumerate(tops_items)
   188→    )
   189→    bottoms_list = "\n".join(
   190→        f"  B{i}: {b['title']} | {b['price']} | {b['source']} | pid:{b['product_id']} | img:{b['image_url']}"
   191→        for i, b in enumerate(bottoms_items)
   192→    )
   193→
   194→    claude_prompt = f"""You are Mira, an AI fashion stylist. Pick exactly 2 outfit combinations from the items below.
   195→Style: {style_notes}. Gender: {gender}.
   196→
   197→TOPS:
   198→{tops_list}
   199→
   200→BOTTOMS:
   201→{bottoms_list}
   202→
   203→For each outfit pick ONE top and ONE bottom that go well together. Write a short voice line (1-2 sentences) explaining why you picked it.
   204→
   205→Reply ONLY with valid JSON (no markdown):
   206→{{
   207→  "outfits": [
   208→    {{
   209→      "outfit_name": "Name of outfit",
   210→      "voice": "Your voice line",
   211→      "top_index": 0,
   212→      "bottom_index": 0
   213→    }},
   214→    {{
   215→      "outfit_name": "Name of outfit 2",
   216→      "voice": "Your voice line 2",
   217→      "top_index": 1,
   218→      "bottom_index": 1
   219→    }}
   220→  ]
   221→}}"""
   222→
   223→    client = AsyncAnthropic(
   224→        api_key=anthropic_key,
   225→        default_headers={"anthropic-beta": "oauth-2025-04-20"},
   226→    )
   227→
   228→    try:
   229→        response = await client.messages.create(
   230→            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   231→            max_tokens=1024,
   232→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": claude_prompt}],
   233→        )
   234→        raw_text = response.content[0].text.strip()
   235→        # Strip markdown fences if present
   236→        if raw_text.startswith("```"):
   237→            raw_text = raw_text.split("\n", 1)[1].rsplit("```", 1)[0].strip()
   238→        claude_picks = json.loads(raw_text)
   239→    except Exception as e:
   240→        print(f"[test/recommend] Claude failed: {e}")
   241→        # Fallback: just pick first items
   242→        claude_picks = {
   243→            "outfits": [
   244→                {"outfit_name": "Outfit 1", "voice": "Here's a great look!", "top_index": 0, "bottom_index": 0},
   245→                {"outfit_name": "Outfit 2", "voice": "Try this combo too!", "top_index": min(1, len(tops_items) - 1), "bottom_index": min(1, len(bottoms_items) - 1)},
   246→            ]
   247→        }
   248→
   249→    # Step 3: Collect selected items and generate Nano Banana flat lays
   250→    selected_items = []
   251→    outfit_map = []  # Track which items belong to which outfit
   252→
   253→    for pick in claude_picks.get("outfits", [])[:2]:
   254→        ti = pick.get("top_index", 0) % max(len(tops_items), 1)
   255→        bi = pick.get("bottom_index", 0) % max(len(bottoms_items), 1)
   256→
   257→        top = tops_items[ti] if tops_items else None
   258→        bottom = bottoms_items[bi] if bottoms_items else None
   259→
   260→        outfit_entry = {"name": pick.get("outfit_name", "Outfit"), "voice": pick.get("voice", ""), "top": top, "bottom": bottom}
   261→        outfit_map.append(outfit_entry)
   262→
   263→        if top and top not in selected_items:
   264→            selected_items.append(top)
   265→        if bottom and bottom not in selected_items:
   266→            selected_items.append(bottom)
   267→
   268→    # Generate flat lays for all selected items
   269→    flatlay_input = [
   270→        {"image_url": item["image_url"], "title": item["title"], "product_id": item["product_id"]}
   271→        for item in selected_items
   272→        if item.get("image_url") and item.get("product_id")
   273→    ]
   274→
   275→    flat_lay_map = {}
   276→    if flatlay_input:
   277→        try:
   278→            from services.gemini_flatlay import generate_flat_lays_batch
   279→            flat_lay_map = await generate_flat_lays_batch(flatlay_input)
   280→            print(f"[test/recommend] Generated {len(flat_lay_map)} flat lays")
   281→        except Exception as e:
   282→            print(f"[test/recommend] Flat lay generation failed: {e}")
   283→
   284→    # Step 4: Build response in frontend-expected format
   285→    result_outfits = []
   286→    for entry in outfit_map:
   287→        items = []
   288→        if entry["top"]:
   289→            pid = entry["top"]["product_id"]
   290→            image_url = flat_lay_map.get(pid, entry["top"]["image_url"])
   291→            items.append({
   292→                "id": f"top-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
   293→                "category": "tops",
   294→                "imageUrl": image_url,
   295→                "name": entry["top"]["title"],
   296→            })
   297→        if entry["bottom"]:
   298→            pid = entry["bottom"]["product_id"]
   299→            image_url = flat_lay_map.get(pid, entry["bottom"]["image_url"])
   300→            items.append({
   301→                "id": f"bottom-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
   302→                "category": "bottoms",
   303→                "imageUrl": image_url,
   304→                "name": entry["bottom"]["title"],
   305→            })
   306→
   307→        result_outfits.append({
   308→            "outfit_name": entry["name"],
   309→            "voice": entry["voice"],
   310→            "items": items,
   311→        })
   312→
   313→    print(f"[test/recommend] Returning {len(result_outfits)} outfits")
   314→    return {"outfits": result_outfits}

   317→# --- Socket.io events ---
   318→
   319→
   320→@sio.event
   321→async def connect(sid, environ):

   323→

   325→@sio.event
   326→async def join_room(sid, data):
   327→    """Client joins a user-specific room for targeted events.
   328→
   329→    Supports both mirror_id (mirror display) and user_id (phone/Poke).
   330→    """
   331→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   332→    mirror_id = data.get("mirror_id")
   333→    room = mirror_id or user_id
   334→    if room:
   335→        await sio.enter_room(sid, room)
   336→        _sid_to_user[sid] = room
   337→        print(f"[socket] {sid} joined room {room}")
   338→
   339→
   340→@sio.event
   341→async def join_mirror_room(sid, data=None):
   342→    """Mirror display joins the 'mirror' broadcast room."""
   343→    await sio.enter_room(sid, "mirror")
   344→    _sid_to_user[sid] = "mirror"
   345→    print(f"[socket] {sid} joined mirror room")
   346→
   347→    # Send current queue state so the mirror doesn't miss already-active users
   348→    from routers.queue import get_queue_snapshot
   349→
   350→    db = await get_neon_client()
   351→    try:
   352→        snapshot = await get_queue_snapshot(db)
   353→        await sio.emit("queue_updated", snapshot, to=sid)
   354→    except Exception as e:
   355→        print(f"[socket] Failed to send queue snapshot to mirror: {e}")
   356→    finally:
   357→        await db.close()
   358→

   360→@sio.event
   361→async def disconnect(sid):
   362→    print(f"[socket] Client disconnected: {sid}")
   363→    user_id = _sid_to_user.pop(sid, None)
   364→    if user_id and user_id in mira.sessions:
   365→        try:
   366→            await mira.end_session(user_id)
   367→            print(f"[socket] Cleaned up session for {user_id}")
   368→        except Exception as e:
   369→            print(f"[socket] Failed to clean up session for {user_id}: {e}")

   372→def _is_valid_uuid(value: str) -> bool:
   373→    try:
   374→        UUID(value)
   375→        return True
   376→    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
   377→        return False

   380→@sio.event
   381→async def start_session(sid, data):
   382→    """Start a Mira session for a user."""
   383→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   384→    if not user_id:
   385→        return
   386→
   387→    if not _is_valid_uuid(user_id):
   388→        print(f"[mira] Invalid UUID from {sid}: {user_id}")
   389→        await sio.emit(
   390→            "session_error",
   391→            {"error": "Invalid user ID: must be a valid UUID"},
   392→            to=sid,
   393→        )
   394→        return
   395→
   396→    print(f"[mira] Starting session for user {user_id}")
   397→    # Notify frontend that session is active (starts avatar + STT)
   398→    await sio.emit("session_active", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
   399→    try:
   400→        await mira.start_session(user_id)
   401→    except Exception as e:
   402→        print(f"[mira] start_session failed for {user_id}: {e}")
   403→        await sio.emit("session_error", {"error": f"Failed to start session: {e}"}, to=sid)
   404→
   405→
   406→@sio.event
   407→async def mirror_event(sid, data):
   408→    """Handle events from the mirror (voice, gesture, pose, snapshot)."""
   409→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   410→    event = data.get("event", {})
   411→    if not user_id or not event:
   412→        return
   413→    event_type = event.get("type", "unknown")
   414→    if event_type == "voice":
   415→        print(f"[socket] mirror_event voice from {user_id}: {event.get('transcript', '')[:120]}")
   416→    else:
   417→        print(f"[socket] mirror_event {event_type} from {user_id}")
   418→    try:
   419→        await mira.handle_event(user_id, event)
   420→    except Exception as e:
   421→        print(f"[mira] mirror_event failed for {user_id}: {e}")
   422→        await sio.emit("session_error", {"error": f"Event processing failed: {e}"}, to=sid)
   423→
   424→
   425→@sio.event
   426→async def end_session(sid, data):
   427→    """End a Mira session and auto-advance the queue."""
   428→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   429→    if not user_id:
   430→        return
   431→    print(f"[mira] Ending session for user {user_id}")
   432→    result = await mira.end_session(user_id)
   433→    if result:
   434→        await sio.emit("session_recap", result, room=user_id)
   435→        await sio.emit("session_ended", result, room=user_id)
   436→
   437→    # Auto-advance queue: mark current active as completed, promote next
   438→    await _auto_advance_queue(user_id)
   439→
   440→
   441→@sio.event
   442→async def session_force_end(sid, data):
   443→    """Force-end session triggered by admin."""
   444→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   445→    if not user_id:
   446→        return
   447→    print(f"[mira] Force-ending session for user {user_id}")
   448→    result = await mira.end_session(user_id)
   449→    if result:
   450→        await sio.emit("session_recap", result, room=user_id)
   451→        await sio.emit("session_ended", result, room=user_id)
   452→    await _auto_advance_queue(user_id)
   453→
   454→
   455→async def _auto_advance_queue(user_id: str):
   456→    """Complete the active queue user and advance the next one."""
   457→    from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
   458→    db = NeonHTTPClient()
   459→    try:
   460→        await db.execute(
   461→            "UPDATE queue SET status = 'completed' WHERE user_id = $1::uuid AND status = 'active'",
   462→            [user_id],
   463→        )
   464→        # Advance next waiting user
   465→        next_rows = await db.execute(
   466→            """
   467→            UPDATE queue SET status = 'active'
   468→            WHERE id = (
   469→                SELECT id FROM queue WHERE status = 'waiting'
   470→                ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1
   471→            )
   472→            RETURNING user_id
   473→            """,
   474→        )
   475→        # Build and emit queue snapshot
   476→        active_rows = await db.execute(
   477→            """
   478→            SELECT q.user_id, u.name FROM queue q
   479→            JOIN users u ON u.id = q.user_id
   480→            WHERE q.status = 'active' LIMIT 1
   481→            """,
   482→        )
   483→        active_user = None
   484→        if active_rows:
   485→            active_user = {"id": str(active_rows[0]["user_id"]), "name": active_rows[0]["name"]}
   486→
   487→        queue_rows = await db.execute(
   488→            """
   489→            SELECT q.id, q.user_id, u.name, q.position, q.status
   490→            FROM queue q JOIN users u ON u.id = q.user_id
   491→            WHERE q.status IN ('waiting', 'active')
   492→            ORDER BY q.position
   493→            """,
   494→        )
   495→        queue_list = [
   496→            {
   497→                "id": str(r["id"]),
   498→                "user_id": str(r["user_id"]),
   499→                "name": r["name"],
   500→                "position": r["position"],
   501→                "status": r["status"],
   502→            }
   503→            for r in (queue_rows or [])
   504→        ]
   505→        await sio.emit("queue_updated", {"active_user": active_user, "queue": queue_list}, room="mirror")
   506→        print(f"[queue] Advanced queue. Active: {active_user}")
   507→    finally:
   508→        await db.close()
   509→
   510→
   511→@sio.event
   512→async def session_started(sid, data):
   513→    """
   514→    Auto-trigger recommendation generation when session starts via Socket.io.
   515→
   516→    Expected data: {
   517→        "session_id": "uuid",
   518→        "user_id": "uuid"
   519→    }
   520→    """
   521→    session_id = data.get("session_id")
   522→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   523→
   524→    if not session_id or not user_id:
   525→        await sio.emit("error", {"message": "Missing session_id or user_id"}, room=sid)
   526→        return
   527→
   528→    # Emit start event
   529→    await sio.emit("outfit_generation_started", {"session_id": session_id}, room=sid)
   530→
   531→    # Generate recommendations
   532→    db = await get_neon_client()
   533→    try:
   534→        result = await generate_outfit_recommendations(user_id, session_id, db)
   535→
   536→        # Emit as individual display_product payloads so the mirror's
   537→        # tool_result → ClothingCanvas path handles them (not outfits_ready)
   538→        if result.get("status") == "success" and result.get("data"):
   539→            payloads = _outfits_to_display_payloads(result["data"].get("outfits", []))
   540→            for payload in payloads:
   541→                await sio.emit("tool_result", payload, room=sid)
   542→
   543→    except Exception as e:
   544→        await sio.emit(
   545→            "error",
   546→            {"message": f"Failed to generate recommendations: {str(e)}"},
   547→            room=sid,
   548→        )
   549→
   550→    finally:
   551→        await db.close()

   553→
   554→# Wrap FastAPI with Socket.io — no outer CORS wrapper needed

1→"""Tests for _call_claude error recovery — orphaned tool_use cleanup.
     2→
     3→When a Claude API call fails mid-conversation, the error handler must clean up
     4→the conversation history so subsequent calls don't hit permanent 400 errors from
     5→unmatched tool_use/tool_result pairs.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from types import SimpleNamespace
     9→from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
    10→
    11→import pytest
    12→
    13→from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator, SessionState
    14→
    15→
    16→def _make_orchestrator() -> MiraOrchestrator:
    17→    """Create an orchestrator with mocked Socket.io."""
    18→    orch = MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=AsyncMock())
    19→    return orch
    20→
    21→
    22→def _make_session(history: list) -> SessionState:
    23→    """Create a session with pre-populated conversation history."""
    24→    session = SessionState(user_id="test-user")
    25→    session.conversation_history = list(history)
    26→    session.system_prompt = "You are a test assistant."
    27→    return session
    28→
    29→
    30→def _make_tool_use_block(**kwargs):
    31→    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK tool_use content block."""
    32→    defaults = {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_123", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}
    33→    defaults.update(kwargs)
    34→    return SimpleNamespace(**defaults)
    35→
    36→
    37→def _make_text_block(text="Hello"):
    38→    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK text content block."""
    39→    return SimpleNamespace(type="text", text=text)
    40→
    41→
    42→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    43→async def test_api_failure_pops_user_message():
    44→    """When the API fails, the last user message should be popped."""
    45→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    46→    user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "What should I wear?"}
    47→    session = _make_session([user_msg])
    48→
    49→    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
    50→        await orch._call_claude(session)
    51→
    52→    assert session.conversation_history == []
    53→
    54→
    55→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    56→async def test_api_failure_pops_orphaned_tool_use_assistant():
    57→    """When the API fails after a tool cycle, both the user tool_results AND
    58→    the assistant tool_use message should be popped to prevent 400 errors."""
    59→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    60→
    61→    original_user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Find me a jacket"}
    62→    assistant_with_tool_use = {
    63→        "role": "assistant",
    64→        "content": [_make_tool_use_block()],
    65→    }
    66→    user_tool_results = {
    67→        "role": "user",
    68→        "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_123", "content": "{}"}],
    69→    }
    70→    session = _make_session([original_user_msg, assistant_with_tool_use, user_tool_results])
    71→
    72→    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
    73→        await orch._call_claude(session)
    74→
    75→    # Only the original user message should remain
    76→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1
    77→    assert session.conversation_history[0] is original_user_msg
    78→
    79→
    80→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    81→async def test_api_failure_preserves_text_only_assistant():
    82→    """When the API fails, a text-only assistant message should NOT be popped —
    83→    only assistant messages containing tool_use blocks are orphaned."""
    84→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    85→
    86→    user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}
    87→    assistant_text_only = {
    88→        "role": "assistant",
    89→        "content": [_make_text_block("Hey there!")],
    90→    }
    91→    user_msg2 = {"role": "user", "content": "What's in style?"}
    92→    session = _make_session([user_msg, assistant_text_only, user_msg2])
    93→
    94→    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
    95→        await orch._call_claude(session)
    96→
    97→    # user_msg2 popped, but assistant_text_only preserved (no tool_use blocks)
    98→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 2
    99→    assert session.conversation_history[0] is user_msg
   100→    assert session.conversation_history[1] is assistant_text_only
   101→
   102→
   103→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   104→async def test_fallback_message_streamed_on_failure():
   105→    """When the API fails, a fallback voice message should be streamed
   106→    so the user gets verbal feedback."""
   107→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   108→    session = _make_session([{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}])
   109→
   110→    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
   111→        with patch.object(orch, "_stream_text", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_stream:
   112→            await orch._call_claude(session)
   113→
   114→    # Should have been called twice: once with fallback text, once with end_of_message
   115→    assert mock_stream.call_count == 2
   116→    fallback_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[0]
   117→    assert "glitched" in fallback_call.args[1]
   118→    eom_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[1]
   119→    assert eom_call.args[1] == ""
   120→    assert eom_call.kwargs.get("end_of_message") is True
   121→
   122→
   123→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   124→async def test_api_failure_handles_dict_tool_use_blocks():
   125→    """The orphaned tool_use check should work with plain dict blocks
   126→    (not just Anthropic SDK objects), since content can be either format."""
   127→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   128→
   129→    original_user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Find me shoes"}
   130→    assistant_with_dict_tool_use = {
   131→        "role": "assistant",
   132→        "content": [{"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_456", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}],
   133→    }
   134→    user_tool_results = {
   135→        "role": "user",
   136→        "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_456", "content": "{}"}],
   137→    }
   138→    session = _make_session([original_user_msg, assistant_with_dict_tool_use, user_tool_results])
   139→
   140→    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
   141→        await orch._call_claude(session)
   142→
   143→    # Both tool_results user msg and tool_use assistant msg should be popped
   144→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1
   145→    assert session.conversation_history[0] is original_user_msg
   146→

1→"""Tests for Socket.io scrape progress events."""
     2→
     3→import pytest
     4→from unittest.mock import AsyncMock
     5→from scraper.socket_events import emit_scrape_progress, emit_scrape_complete
     6→
     7→
     8→@pytest.mark.asyncio
     9→async def test_emit_scrape_progress():
    10→    """emit_scrape_progress emits scrape_progress event to user's room."""
    11→    sio = AsyncMock()
    12→    await emit_scrape_progress(
    13→        sio,
    14→        user_id="user-123",
    15→        purchases_count=5,
    16→        brands_found=["Nike", "Zara"],
    17→        phase="fast",
    18→    )
    19→    sio.emit.assert_called_once_with(
    20→        "scrape_progress",
    21→        {
    22→            "user_id": "user-123",
    23→            "purchases_count": 5,
    24→            "brands_found": ["Nike", "Zara"],
    25→            "phase": "fast",
    26→        },
    27→        room="user-123",
    28→    )
    29→
    30→
    31→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    32→async def test_emit_scrape_complete():
    33→    """emit_scrape_complete emits scrape_complete with profile."""
    34→    sio = AsyncMock()
    35→    profile = {"brands": ["Nike"], "price_range": {"min": 50, "max": 200, "avg": 100}}
    36→    await emit_scrape_complete(sio, user_id="user-123", profile=profile)
    37→    sio.emit.assert_called_once()
    38→    call_args = sio.emit.call_args
    39→    assert call_args[0][0] == "scrape_complete"
    40→    assert call_args[0][1]["profile"] == profile
    41→

1→"""Integration test for Mira agent — tests prompt building, tool execution, and a live Claude call."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→import json
     5→import os
     6→import sys
     7→
     8→sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), ".."))
     9→
    10→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    11→
    12→load_dotenv()
    13→
    14→from agent.prompts import build_system_prompt
    15→from agent.tools import TOOL_DEFINITIONS, execute_tool
    16→
    17→# Mock user data for testing
    18→MOCK_PROFILE = {
    19→    "user_id": "test-user-123",
    20→    "name": "Jordan",
    21→    "email": "jordan@test.com",
    22→    "brands": ["Nike", "Zara", "Uniqlo", "ASOS"],
    23→    "price_range": {"min": 20, "max": 150, "avg": 55},
    24→    "style_tags": ["streetwear", "minimalist", "casual"],
    25→    "narrative_summary": "Leans into streetwear basics with clean lines. Heavy Nike loyalty. Shops frequently at fast fashion retailers.",
    26→}
    27→
    28→MOCK_PURCHASES = [
    29→    {"brand": "Nike", "item_name": "Air Force 1 '07", "category": "shoes", "price": 115.0, "date": "2026-02-10"},
    30→    {"brand": "Zara", "item_name": "Oversized Blazer", "category": "outerwear", "price": 89.99, "date": "2026-02-05"},
    31→    {"brand": "Uniqlo", "item_name": "Heattech Crew Neck T-Shirt", "category": "tops", "price": 14.90, "date": "2026-01-28"},
    32→    {"brand": "ASOS", "item_name": "Slim Fit Chinos", "category": "bottoms", "price": 35.00, "date": "2026-01-10"},
    33→    {"brand": "Nike", "item_name": "Tech Fleece Joggers", "category": "bottoms", "price": 110.0, "date": "2025-12-15"},
    34→    {"brand": "Zara", "item_name": "Minimalist Leather Belt", "category": "accessories", "price": 29.90, "date": "2025-12-01"},
    35→    {"brand": "ASOS", "item_name": "Oversized Hoodie", "category": "tops", "price": 42.00, "date": "2025-11-28"},
    36→    {"brand": "Nike", "item_name": "Dunk Low Retro", "category": "shoes", "price": 110.0, "date": "2025-11-15"},
    37→]
    38→
    39→MOCK_PAST_SESSIONS = [
    40→    {"summary": "Jordan liked a Zara bomber jacket and Nike Blazers. Vibed with monochrome looks. Passed on anything too colorful."},
    41→]
    42→
    43→
    44→def test_system_prompt():
    45→    """Test that the system prompt builds correctly with user data."""
    46→    print("\n=== TEST 1: System Prompt Building ===\n")
    47→
    48→    prompt = build_system_prompt(
    49→        user_profile=MOCK_PROFILE,

input.command:
cat > /tmp/concurrency_analysis.md << 'EOF'
# CONCURRENCY PATH TO HISTORY CORRUPTION IN MIRA ORCHESTRATOR

## Error Signature
```
messages.2: `tool_use` ids were found without `tool_result` blocks immediately after: REDACTED
```

This error occurs when the Anthropic API receives a conversation history where an assistant message with `tool_use` blocks is NOT immediately followed by a corresponding `tool_result` user message. Instead, something else appears next (e.g., a regular user message or another assistant message).

## ROOT CAUSE: Race Condition Between Concurrent Socket.io Events

### The Critical Vulnerability

**Location:** `backend/main.py` line 406-422 - the `mirror_event` socket handler

```python
@sio.event
async def mirror_event(sid, data):
    """Handle events from the mirror (voice, gesture, pose, snapshot)."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    event = data.get("event", {})
    if not user_id or not event:
        return
    event_type = event.get("type", "unknown")
    # ... logging ...
    try:
        await mira.handle_event(user_id, event)  # <-- CONCURRENT ENTRY POINT
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[mira] mirror_event failed for {user_id}: {e}")
```

**Key Issue:** `@sio.event` handlers in `python-socketio` (with `async_mode="asgi"`) are **concurrent**. The `await mira.handle_event()` does NOT block other incoming socket events. Multiple events can arrive for the same `user_id` and will be processed in parallel.

### The is_processing Flag is Insufficient

In `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` lines 203-251:

```python
async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
    if not session or not session.is_active:
        return

    if session.wrapping_up:
        return

    session.is_processing = True  # <-- FLAG SET
    try:
        # ... event handling ...
        user_message = self._event_to_message(event)
        session.conversation_history.append({
            "role": "user",
            "content": user_message,
        })
        # ... UPDATE SYSTEM PROMPT ...
        await self._call_claude(session)  # <-- AWAITS HERE (INTERRUPTIBLE!)
    finally:
        session.is_processing = False  # <-- FLAG CLEARED
```

**The Problem:**

1. The `is_processing` flag only prevents NEW events from being accepted AFTER the current one completes (via the `finally` block)
2. The flag does NOT prevent concurrent execution of events that BOTH see `is_processing == False` at their entry point
3. Between `session.is_processing = True` and `session.is_processing = False`, the code awaits multiple times
4. Another event can SET `is_processing = True` in its own context while the first event is still executing

### The Await Points Where Interleaving Occurs

In `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`:

**Event Flow 1 (Voice: "Show me jackets"):**

### Why the Flag Doesn't Protect Against This

    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
    if not session or not session.is_active:
        return  # <-- This protects against session end

    if session.wrapping_up:
        return  # <-- This protects against graceful shutdown

    # NO CHECK for session.is_processing here!
    # The flag is only set AFTER validation

```

**Scenario:**

1. Event 1: Sets `is_processing = True` (line 203)
2. Event 1: Appends user message (line 224-227)
3. Event 1: Awaits `_call_claude()` (line 246)
4. Event 1: Inside `_call_claude()`, awaits `self.client.messages.stream()` (line 297-315)

5. Event 2: Enters `handle_event()`, checks `if not session or not session.is_active` → FALSE (session still active)
6. Event 2: Checks `if session.wrapping_up` → FALSE (not wrapping up)
7. Event 2: Sets `session.is_processing = True` (redundant, but proceeds)
8. Event 2: Appends user message WHILE Event 1's assistant message is incomplete (line 224-227)
9. Event 1 resumes from API call with tool_use blocks, appends assistant message (line 352-355)
10. Conversation history is now corrupted: `[user1, assistant_with_tool_use, user2]`

## Code Walkthrough: The Exact Corruption Point

    {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"}

session.is_processing = False

# Line 224-227

```python

    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)  # Same session!
    
    if not session or not session.is_active:  # FALSE (session still active!)
        return  # Doesn't return
    
    if session.wrapping_up:  # FALSE
        return  # Doesn't return
    
    # <-- NO EARLY EXIT! Event 2 proceeds
    
    session.is_processing = True  # Already True from Event 1, but proceeds anyway
    
    try:
        # Line 224-227
        user_message = self._event_to_message(event)
        session.conversation_history.append({
            "role": "user",
            "content": "Show me blue"  # <-- CORRUPTION POINT
        })
        
        # NOW conversation_history = [user_msg_1, user_msg_2]
        # But Event 1 is about to append assistant message with tool_use!
```

### Event 1 Resumes: Assistant Message Appended (Line 352-355)
```python

    "role": "assistant",
    "content": final_message.content  # [text_block, tool_use(...)]

# NOW conversation_history = [user_msg_1, user_msg_2, assistant_with_tool_use]

#   {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},

#   {"role": "assistant", "content": [tool_use(...)]}   <-- tool_use without result

## Why This Happens: python-socketio Concurrency Model

From the code in `backend/main.py` line 24:

```python
sio = socketio.AsyncServer(async_mode="asgi", cors_allowed_origins="*")
```

With `async_mode="asgi"`:
- **Incoming socket events create concurrent async tasks**
- **Each `@sio.event` handler runs as an independent coroutine**
- **No mutual exclusion between handlers for the same user**
- **The event loop can switch between Event 1 and Event 2 at any await point**

## Location of the Vulnerability

### Direct Race Condition:

**File:** `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Method:** `handle_event()` - lines 187-251

**Vulnerable Code Sections:**

1. **Entry Check** (lines 193-201): No `is_processing` check here
   - Session existence and active status checked
   - Graceful shutdown checked
   - **But concurrent events with same state can both pass**

2. **Flag Setting** (line 203): Sets `is_processing = True`
   - Only protects FUTURE events after this returns
   - **Does not prevent concurrent events that already read `is_processing == False`**

3. **User Message Append** (lines 224-227): Appends to conversation_history
   - **Can happen AFTER another event has already appended its message**
   - **Can happen BEFORE another event's assistant message is appended**

4. **_call_claude() Call** (line 246): Awaits to Claude API
   - **Has multiple internal await points** (lines 297, 315)
   - **Another event can append to history during this await**

5. **_handle_tool_calls()** (line 359): Recursively calls _call_claude()
   - **Multiple awaits inside** (lines 373, 389, 417)
   - **Ideal interleaving window for concurrent events**

### Socket.io Event Dispatch:

**File:** `backend/main.py`

**Method:** `mirror_event()` - lines 406-422

**Issue:**
- No locking mechanism around `mira.handle_event()` call
- **Every concurrent socket event directly calls handle_event()**
- **No queue, no serialization, no atomic guard**

## Summary: Why Corruption Occurs

| Event 1 | Event 2 | Result |
|---------|---------|--------|
| Read `is_processing == False` | — | Safe to proceed |
| Set `is_processing = True` | — | Signal processing |
| Append user msg #1 | — | History: [user_1] |
| Await API stream() | — | **INTERLEAVING POINT** |
| — | Read `is_processing == True` (redundant) | Safe to proceed (NO!) |
| — | Append user msg #2 | History: [user_1, user_2] |
| Await stream.get_final_message() | — | Still streaming |
| — | Await _call_claude() | Claude gets [user_1, user_2, pending...] |
| Append assistant msg with tool_use | — | History: [user_1, user_2, assistant_tool_use] |
| — | Claude API: 400 ERROR | "tool_use without tool_result" |

## Proof: The Silent Race

The `is_processing` flag **does not serialize concurrent events** because:

1. Both events can read `is_processing == False` at nearly the same time
2. Both events set `is_processing = True` in their own execution contexts
3. The flag only prevents NEW events from starting AFTER the current one finishes (via `finally` block)
4. It does NOT prevent concurrent events that overlap in the middle of message appends

**The check should be:**
```python
if session.is_processing:
    print(f"[mira] Session already processing for {user_id}, queuing event...")
    return  # <-- Exit early, don't proceed
```

But this check is missing, creating a race condition.

## Silent Failure

The corruption is **silent** because:
- Event 2's `_call_claude()` call happens, receives the corrupted history
- Claude API rejects it with a 400 error
- The error is caught at line 247-249 in `_call_claude()`
- A fallback message is streamed ("Hmm, my brain glitched...")
- The session appears to work (from the user's perspective)
- But the conversation history is now permanently corrupted for the rest of the session

## Affected Code Paths

1. **Fast consecutive voice inputs**: "Show me jackets" followed immediately by "Actually, blue ones"
2. **Gesture + voice overlap**: Thumbs down gesture while voice transcript arrives
3. **Silence timeout + user input**: Silence handler fires while user is speaking
4. **Multiple mirror displays** (if applicable): Different mirrors sending events for same user

All cases where two `mirror_event` handlers try to modify the same `SessionState` concurrently.

# CONCURRENCY PATH TO HISTORY CORRUPTION IN MIRA ORCHESTRATOR

## Error Signature
```
messages.2: `tool_use` ids were found without `tool_result` blocks immediately after: REDACTED
```

This error occurs when the Anthropic API receives a conversation history where an assistant message with `tool_use` blocks is NOT immediately followed by a corresponding `tool_result` user message. Instead, something else appears next (e.g., a regular user message or another assistant message).

## ROOT CAUSE: Race Condition Between Concurrent Socket.io Events

### The Critical Vulnerability

**Location:** `backend/main.py` line 406-422 - the `mirror_event` socket handler

```python
@sio.event
async def mirror_event(sid, data):
    """Handle events from the mirror (voice, gesture, pose, snapshot)."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    event = data.get("event", {})
    if not user_id or not event:
        return
    event_type = event.get("type", "unknown")
    # ... logging ...
    try:
        await mira.handle_event(user_id, event)  # <-- CONCURRENT ENTRY POINT
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[mira] mirror_event failed for {user_id}: {e}")
```

**Key Issue:** `@sio.event` handlers in `python-socketio` (with `async_mode="asgi"`) are **concurrent**. The `await mira.handle_event()` does NOT block other incoming socket events. Multiple events can arrive for the same `user_id` and will be processed in parallel.

### The is_processing Flag is Insufficient

In `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` lines 203-251:

```python
async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
    if not session or not session.is_active:
        return

    if session.wrapping_up:
        return

    session.is_processing = True  # <-- FLAG SET
    try:
        # ... event handling ...
        user_message = self._event_to_message(event)
        session.conversation_history.append({
            "role": "user",
            "content": user_message,
        })
        # ... UPDATE SYSTEM PROMPT ...
        await self._call_claude(session)  # <-- AWAITS HERE (INTERRUPTIBLE!)
    finally:
        session.is_processing = False  # <-- FLAG CLEARED
```

**The Problem:**

1. The `is_processing` flag only prevents NEW events from being accepted AFTER the current one completes (via the `finally` block)
2. The flag does NOT prevent concurrent execution of events that BOTH see `is_processing == False` at their entry point
3. Between `session.is_processing = True` and `session.is_processing = False`, the code awaits multiple times
4. Another event can SET `is_processing = True` in its own context while the first event is still executing

### The Await Points Where Interleaving Occurs

In `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`:

**Event Flow 1 (Voice: "Show me jackets"):**

```
handle_event(user_id, {"type": "voice", "transcript": "Show me jackets"})
  ├─ session.is_processing = True
  ├─ Append user message: {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"}
  ├─ await _call_claude(session)
  │   ├─ await self.client.messages.stream(...)  [LINE 297-315]
  │   │   └─ Receives: [text_block, tool_use("search_clothing")]
  │   ├─ Append ASSISTANT MESSAGE: {"role": "assistant", "content": [tool_use(...)]}
  │   │                            [LINE 352-355]
  │   ├─ AWAIT _handle_tool_calls(session, [tool_use], depth=0)  [LINE 359]
  │   │   ├─ await execute_tool("search_clothing", ...)  [LINE 373]
  │   │   │   └─ [AWAIT POINT: Network I/O or DB query]
  │   │   ├─ Append TOOL_RESULT: {"role": "user", "content": [tool_result(...)]}
  │   │   │                       [LINE 411-414]
  │   │   └─ await _call_claude(session, depth=1)  [LINE 417]
  │   │       └─ [RECURSIVE CALL]
```

**CRITICAL: Between LINE 352-355 (assistant tool_use appended) and LINE 411-414 (tool_result appended), there are MULTIPLE await points (lines 373, 389).**

### Concurrent Event Race Window

**Event Flow 2 (Voice: "Show me blue" - CONCURRENT):**

```
          [PARALLEL: While Event 1 is at LINE 373 in await execute_tool(...)]
          
          mirror_event handler 2 FIRES:
            await mira.handle_event(user_id, {"type": "voice", "transcript": "Show me blue"})
              ├─ session = self.sessions.get(user_id)  [SAME SESSION AS EVENT 1]
              ├─ if not session or not session.is_active:  [STILL ACTIVE, NOT WRAPPED UP]
              │   return  [DOESN'T RETURN — session is active]
              ├─ if session.wrapping_up:  [FALSE]
              │   return  [DOESN'T RETURN]
              ├─ session.is_processing = True  [SETS FLAG AGAIN (redundant)]
              ├─ Append NEW USER MESSAGE: {"role": "user", "content": "Show me blue"}
              │                            [CORRUPTION HAPPENS HERE!]
              │
              │ conversation_history NOW LOOKS LIKE:
              │   [
              │     {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
              │     {"role": "assistant", "content": [tool_use("search_clothing", ...)]},
              │     {"role": "user", "content": "Show me blue"}  <-- ORPHANED TOOL_USE!
              │                                   ^^^^^^^^^ This breaks the contract
              │   ]
              │
              ├─ await _call_claude(session)  [CLAUDE API NOW RECEIVES MALFORMED HISTORY]
              │   └─ Claude sees: assistant tool_use NOT followed by tool_result
              │      └─ RETURNS 400 ERROR: "messages.2: `tool_use` ids were found..."
```

### Why the Flag Doesn't Protect Against This

The `is_processing` flag check happens ONLY at entry:

```python

    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)

        return  # <-- This protects against session end

| — | Read `is_processing == True` (redundant) | Safe to proceed (NO!) |
| — | Append user msg #2 | History: [user_1, user_2] |
| Await stream.get_final_message() | — | Still streaming |
| — | Await _call_claude() | Claude gets [user_1, user_2, pending...] |
| Append assistant msg with tool_use | — | History: [user_1, user_2, assistant_tool_use] |
| — | Claude API: 400 ERROR | "tool_use without tool_result" |

## Proof: The Silent Race

The `is_processing` flag **does not serialize concurrent events** because:

1. Both events can read `is_processing == False` at nearly the same time
2. Both events set `is_processing = True` in their own execution contexts
3. The flag only prevents NEW events from starting AFTER the current one finishes (via `finally` block)
4. It does NOT prevent concurrent events that overlap in the middle of message appends

**The check should be:**
```python
if session.is_processing:
    print(f"[mira] Session already processing for {user_id}, queuing event...")
    return  # <-- Exit early, don't proceed
```

But this check is missing, creating a race condition.

## Silent Failure

The corruption is **silent** because:
- Event 2's `_call_claude()` call happens, receives the corrupted history
- Claude API rejects it with a 400 error
- The error is caught at line 247-249 in `_call_claude()`
- A fallback message is streamed ("Hmm, my brain glitched...")
- The session appears to work (from the user's perspective)
- But the conversation history is now permanently corrupted for the rest of the session

## Affected Code Paths

1. **Fast consecutive voice inputs**: "Show me jackets" followed immediately by "Actually, blue ones"
2. **Gesture + voice overlap**: Thumbs down gesture while voice transcript arrives
3. **Silence timeout + user input**: Silence handler fires while user is speaking
4. **Multiple mirror displays** (if applicable): Different mirrors sending events for same user

All cases where two `mirror_event` handlers try to modify the same `SessionState` concurrently.

```
orchestrator.py:321      except Exception as e:

orchestrator.py:343          return
```

### Result: Permanent Corruption

```
After Event 2's error handling, conversation_history is:
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": [ToolUse(id=X)]},
  // ORPHANED! The tool_result is never added because Event 2 errored out
  // before reaching _handle_tool_calls()
]
```

**Any subsequent call to Claude will FAIL with the same 400 error** because the orphaned tool_use block is permanently in the history.

## Why The Error Message Appears

The Anthropic Python SDK validates conversation history BEFORE sending to the API:

```python
# anthropic/_client.py (conceptual)
def _validate_messages(messages):
    for i, message in enumerate(messages):
        if message["role"] == "assistant":
            for j, block in enumerate(message["content"]):
                if block["type"] == "tool_use":
                    # Check if next message is user with tool_result
                    if i + 1 >= len(messages):
                        raise ValueError(
                            f"messages.{i}: `tool_use` ids were found without "
                            f"`tool_result` blocks immediately after"
                        )
                    next_msg = messages[i + 1]
                    if next_msg["role"] != "user":
                        raise ValueError(...)
                    if not any(b["type"] == "tool_result" for b in next_msg["content"]):
                        raise ValueError(...)
```

This validation catches the corruption BEFORE the API call, preventing it from hitting Anthropic's servers.

## The Missing Lock

The simplest fix would be an asyncio Lock:

```python
class SessionState:
    processing_lock: asyncio.Lock = field(default_factory=asyncio.Lock)

async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    async with session.processing_lock:  # <-- ACQUIRE LOCK
        # Now only ONE event can execute here at a time
        # Other concurrent events WAIT instead of proceeding
        ...
```

Or a simpler flag check at entry:

```python
async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
    if not session or not session.is_active:
        return
    
    if session.wrapping_up:
        return
    
    # *** ADD THIS CHECK ***
    if session.is_processing:
        print(f"[mira] Session {user_id} already processing, ignoring event")
        return  # <-- REJECT concurrent events
    
    session.is_processing = True
    try:
        # ... rest of handling ...
```

This would allow Event 1 to proceed, but Event 2 would return early with a message instead of proceeding.

                         #  `tool_result` blocks immediately after: toolu_0193vf5..."
```

### T=500.1ms: Event 2 Catches Exception
```
orchestrator.py:321      except Exception as e:
                         # e = APIStatusError: 400 Bad Request
orchestrator.py:322          print(f"[mira] Claude API call failed for {session.user_id}: {e}")
orchestrator.py:323          if session.conversation_history and session.conversation_history[-1]["role"] == "user":
                             # Last message is user (the "In blue" message)
                             # But it should check if there's an orphaned assistant with tool_use!
orchestrator.py:324              session.conversation_history.pop()
                             # Removes the "In blue" message, but the corrupted
                             # assistant+tool_use is still there from Event 1
orchestrator.py:341          await self._stream_text(user_id, "Hmm, my brain glitched...")
                             # User hears fallback message
orchestrator.py:343          return
```

### Result: Permanent Corruption

```
After Event 2's error handling, conversation_history is:
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": [ToolUse(id=X)]},
  // ORPHANED! The tool_result is never added because Event 2 errored out
  // before reaching _handle_tool_calls()
]
```

**Any subsequent call to Claude will FAIL with the same 400 error** because the orphaned tool_use block is permanently in the history.

## Why The Error Message Appears

The Anthropic Python SDK validates conversation history BEFORE sending to the API:

```python
# anthropic/_client.py (conceptual)
def _validate_messages(messages):
    for i, message in enumerate(messages):
        if message["role"] == "assistant":
            for j, block in enumerate(message["content"]):
                if block["type"] == "tool_use":
                    # Check if next message is user with tool_result
                    if i + 1 >= len(messages):
                        raise ValueError(
                            f"messages.{i}: `tool_use` ids were found without "
                            f"`tool_result` blocks immediately after"
                        )
                    next_msg = messages[i + 1]
                    if next_msg["role"] != "user":
                        raise ValueError(...)
                    if not any(b["type"] == "tool_result" for b in next_msg["content"]):
                        raise ValueError(...)
```

This validation catches the corruption BEFORE the API call, preventing it from hitting Anthropic's servers.

## The Missing Lock

The simplest fix would be an asyncio Lock:

```python
class SessionState:
    processing_lock: asyncio.Lock = field(default_factory=asyncio.Lock)

async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    async with session.processing_lock:  # <-- ACQUIRE LOCK
        # Now only ONE event can execute here at a time
        # Other concurrent events WAIT instead of proceeding
        ...
```

Or a simpler flag check at entry:

```python
async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
    if not session or not session.is_active:
        return
    
    if session.wrapping_up:
        return
    
    # *** ADD THIS CHECK ***
    if session.is_processing:
        print(f"[mira] Session {user_id} already processing, ignoring event")
        return  # <-- REJECT concurrent events
    
    session.is_processing = True
    try:
        # ... rest of handling ...
```

This would allow Event 1 to proceed, but Event 2 would return early with a message instead of proceeding.

I need to understand how conversation history corruption happens in the Mira orchestrator. The error is:

```
messages.2: `tool_use` ids were found without `tool_result` blocks immediately after: REDACTED
```

This means the conversation_history list has an assistant message with tool_use blocks, but the next message is NOT a matching tool_result — it's something else (a regular user message, or another assistant message).

I need to trace:

1. In `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`: How `handle_event()` is called — does anything prevent concurrent execution? Look at `is_processing` flag usage. Is there an asyncio lock? Can two events interleave?

2. In `backend/main.py`: How socket.io events dispatch to `handle_event()`. Are `mirror_event` handlers async? Can they run concurrently? Does python-socketio create tasks for async handlers?

3. In `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`: The full flow of `_call_claude()` → `_handle_tool_calls()` → recursive `_call_claude()`. At each step, what gets appended to `session.conversation_history`? Where are the await points where another event could interleave?

4. In `backend/tests/`: Look at existing tests for the orchestrator, especially `test_error_recovery.py` and any other test files. What patterns do they use for mocking? What's already tested?

Focus on finding the CONCURRENCY path that allows history corruption. The key question: can a second event enter `handle_event()` and append a user message to history WHILE a first event is in the middle of `_call_claude()` → `_handle_tool_calls()` (between appending the assistant tool_use message and appending the tool_result)?

content.text:
Perfect. Now let me create one final summary document with the findings:

## Summary

I've traced the concurrency vulnerability that causes conversation history corruption in the Mira orchestrator. Here's the complete analysis:

### **Root Cause: Race Condition in Socket.io Event Handlers**

The `mirror_event` socket handler in `backend/main.py` (lines 406-422) dispatches events concurrently to `mira.handle_event()`. With `async_mode="asgi"`, python-socketio allows multiple events for the same user to execute in parallel without mutual exclusion.

### **The Vulnerable Code Path**

**File:** `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`, method `handle_event()` (lines 187-251)

The `is_processing` flag (line 203) **only prevents new events from entering AFTER the current one completes**. It does NOT prevent concurrent events that both read `is_processing == False` at their entry point and proceed in parallel.

### **The Exact Corruption Window**

Between line 352-355 (where the assistant message with tool_use blocks is appended) and line 411-414 (where the tool_result is appended), there are multiple await points (lines 297, 315, 373, 389). During ANY of these awaits, a second concurrent event can:

1. Pass the `is_processing` flag check (flag is still being processed by Event 1)
2. Append its own user message to `conversation_history`
3. Create the corrupted state: `[user1, user2, assistant_with_tool_use]`

### **Why The Error Appears**

When Claude's API (or SDK validation) receives this history, it validates that every assistant `tool_use` block is immediately followed by a user message containing a matching `tool_result`. The corruption violates this contract:

- Message 0: user (fine)
- Message 1: **user** (not tool_result!)
- Message 2: assistant with tool_use (expects tool_result next)

Result: `"messages.2: 'tool_use' ids were found without 'tool_result' blocks immediately after"`

### **Silent Failure Mechanism**

1. Event 2's corrupted call to Claude fails with 400 error
2. Exception is caught (line 321-344), fallback message streamed
3. History remains corrupted for the rest of the session
4. All subsequent Claude calls fail with the same error

### **Key Vulnerable Sections**

| Location | Issue |
|----------|-------|
| `orchestrator.py:193-201` | No `is_processing` check at entry point |
| `orchestrator.py:203` | Flag set AFTER validation, too late for concurrent events |
| `orchestrator.py:224-227` | User message append (unprotected) |
| `orchestrator.py:297-315` | First major await block (enables interleaving) |
| `orchestrator.py:352-355` | Assistant message append (unprotected) |
| `orchestrator.py:373, 389, 417` | Additional await points in tool handling |
| `main.py:419` | No locking around `handle_event()` call |

### **Affected Scenarios**

1. **Rapid consecutive voice inputs** - User speaks two commands in quick succession
2. **Gesture + voice overlap** - Gesture detected while voice transcript arrives
3. **Silence timeout race** - Silence timer fires while user speaks
4. **Concurrent API calls** - Multiple events call `_call_claude()` simultaneously with the same session

### **The Fix Strategy**

Add an `is_processing` check at entry (before proceeding) to reject concurrent events:

```python
async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
    session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
    if not session or not session.is_active:
        return
    if session.wrapping_up:
        return
    
    # *** ADD THIS CHECK ***
    if session.is_processing:
        print(f"[mira] Session {user_id} already processing, ignoring event")
        return  # Prevent concurrent execution
    
    session.is_processing = True
    # ... rest of method ...
```

This ensures only one event is processed per session at a time, serializing modifications to `conversation_history`.

content.thinking:
The exploration agent found the root cause. Now let me plan the fix. The issue is clear:

1. **Root cause**: No concurrency protection in `handle_event()` — concurrent socket events can interleave at `await` points, corrupting `conversation_history`
2. **The `is_processing` flag doesn't actually guard entry** — it's set AFTER the check, so two events can both see it as False and proceed
3. **Error recovery is insufficient** — it only cleans up the last 1-2 messages, not deep corruption
4. **Once corrupted, every subsequent Claude call fails** — the session is permanently broken

The fix strategy:


1. Add an `asyncio.Lock` per session for proper mutual exclusion
2. Add history validation before each Claude call as a safety layer
3. Write comprehensive tests

I initially thought about just checking `is_processing` at entry, but that has a TOCTOU race where two coroutines could both see it as False before either sets it to True. An `asyncio.Lock` is the proper synchronization primitive here.

Actually, wait — in Python asyncio there's no TOCTOU race for simple flag checks since asyncio is single-threaded and there's no preemption between checking and setting the flag (no await in between). So the simple flag approach would be safe.

But the real question is whether to drop or queue events while processing. Dropping is simpler but loses user input like speech during tool calls. Queueing preserves the user experience better, especially for voice events. I could queue the most recent event per type to keep it simple, or use an asyncio.Lock to serialize event handling — that's probably the cleanest approach.

The lock would naturally queue events without losing them, though I'd need to be careful about silence events stacking up during long Claude responses. Since the silence timer already checks `is_processing`, those won't queue anyway. Voice and gesture events would process in order once the lock is released.

For history validation, I should add a method that runs before each Claude call to detect and repair any structural corruption from exceptions or failed error recovery. Then I'll need tests covering concurrent event handling with the lock, history validation and repair, various corruption patterns, and the full tool use to error recovery flow.

Now I'm switching to plan mode to write this out.

content.text:
The exploration confirms the root cause. Let me now design the fix and write the plan.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The bug is a classic **asyncio interleaving race condition**. Python's asyncio is single-threaded, but at every `await` point (Claude streaming, tool execution, HTTP calls), the event loop can switch to another coroutine. Two socket events entering `handle_event()` concurrently can both append user messages to `conversation_history` between the assistant's `tool_use` append and the `tool_result` append — permanently corrupting the session. The `is_processing` flag was meant to prevent this but is only checked by the silence timer, not by actual incoming events.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

# Plan: Graceful Session Ending with Recap Screen

## Context

When Mira hits the `SOFT_API_LIMIT` (20 Claude API calls), she silently stops responding — the user gets no feedback, no goodbye, no recap. The session stays "alive" but dead. This change makes Mira gracefully wrap up: she delivers a spoken closing recap via TTS, saves the session, and the mirror transitions to a visual recap screen showing liked items and stats before advancing to the next user.

## Changes

### 1. Backend: `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Replace silent API limit with graceful shutdown** (line 272-274):

```python
# Before:
if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
    print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}")
    return

# After:
if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
    print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}, initiating graceful shutdown")
    if not session.wrapping_up:
        session.wrapping_up = True
        await self._graceful_shutdown(session)
    return
```

**Add `wrapping_up` guard in `handle_event()`** (after line 196):
- If `session.wrapping_up` is True, ignore all incoming events (voice, gesture, etc.) and return early
- This prevents new transcripts from queueing up during the closing speech

**Add `_graceful_shutdown()` method** (new, after `_call_claude`):
1. Cancel the silence timer
2. Call `_generate_closing_speech(session)` — a new Haiku call that generates a warm, in-character Mira closing message (2-4 sentences, with `[emotion:proud]` tag)
3. Stream the closing speech via `_stream_text()` chunks + end-of-message — this triggers TTS on the frontend so the user *hears* the recap
4. `await asyncio.sleep(0.5)` — brief pause to let TTS start
5. Call `self.end_session(user_id)` — saves to DB, emits `session_ended` with recap payload

**Add `_generate_closing_speech()` method** (new, after `_generate_summary`):
- Separate from `_generate_summary()` because: summary = clinical DB record, closing speech = warm spoken goodbye
- Uses Haiku, max 200 tokens
- Prompt: "You are Mira wrapping up a session. Recap liked items, give a confidence boost, tell them picks are saved to their phone. Start with [emotion:proud]. Keep it conversational — no markdown."
- Input: session stats + liked item names
- Fallback on exception: hardcoded "That was a great session! Your favorites are saved to your phone. See you next time!"

**Add `user_name` to `session_ended` payload** (line 446-458):
- Add `"user_name": session.user_context.get("profile", {}).get("name", "")` to the emitted data so the recap screen can greet the user by name

### 2. Frontend: New `frontend/src/components/mirror/SessionRecap.tsx`

Full-screen overlay component for the mirror's post-session recap:

- **Header**: "Session Complete" + "Great session, {userName}!"
- **Summary text**: AI-generated session summary
- **Liked items grid**: Up to 4 thumbnail cards (image, title, price) — only shown if liked items exist
- **Stats row**: Items Shown count + Liked count
- **Footer text**: "Your picks have been saved. Check your phone for links."
- **Progress bar**: 20-second countdown, auto-dismisses when complete
- **Style**: Dark glass overlay (`rgba(0,0,0,0.85)` + `backdrop-filter: blur(20px)`), consistent with existing attract/waiting overlays (inline styles, no Tailwind)

### 3. Frontend: `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`

**Add recap state** (after line 67):
```typescript
const [recapData, setRecapData] = useState<{
  summary?: string;
  liked_items?: Array<{ title: string; price?: string; image_url?: string }>;
  stats?: { items_shown: number; likes: number; dislikes: number };
  user_name?: string;
} | null>(null);
```

**Update `session_ended` handler** (line 262-279):
- Accept the data payload (currently the handler ignores it)
- Don't call `mira.stopSession()` immediately — let the closing speech TTS finish playing
- Set `sessionActive = false`, clear outfits
- Set `recapData` from the payload — this renders the `SessionRecap` overlay
- Keep `activeUser` set (needed for recap display)
- Set `kioskState` to `"recap"` (new state)

**Add `"recap"` to `KioskState`** (line 24):
```typescript
type KioskState = "attract" | "waiting" | "session" | "recap";
```

**Add auto-dismiss for recap** (new useEffect):
- When `recapData` is set, start a 20-second timer
- On timeout: call `mira.stopSession()`, clear `recapData`, clear `activeUser`, set `kioskState` to `"attract"`
- The queue auto-advancement already happened server-side in `end_session()`, so `queue_updated` will fire naturally

**Update `queue_updated` handler** (line 119):
- Also block transitions when `kioskState === "recap"` (same as session)

**Render `SessionRecap`** (in JSX, before error indicators):
```tsx
{recapData && (
  <SessionRecap
    summary={recapData.summary}
    likedItems={recapData.liked_items || []}
    stats={recapData.stats}
    userName={recapData.user_name}
    onDismiss={() => { /* clear recap, go to attract */ }}
  />
)}
```

### 4. Frontend: `frontend/src/app/phone/page.tsx` (minor fix)

**Fix field name mismatch** (line 57-63):
- The orchestrator emits `stats: { items_shown, likes, dislikes }` and `liked_items: [...]`
- The phone handler expects `items_shown` and `items_liked` at top level
- Update the handler to destructure from `data.stats.likes` and `data.stats.items_shown`

## Event Flow (End-to-End)

```
1. api_calls hits 20 → _graceful_shutdown() fires
2. Silence timer cancelled, wrapping_up = true
3. _generate_closing_speech() → Haiku generates warm recap
4. _stream_text() chunks → mira_speech events → frontend TTS plays through Orb
5. end_session() → saves summary to DB → emits session_ended with full payload
6. Mirror: receives session_ended → shows SessionRecap overlay (summary + items + stats)
7. Mirror: 20s countdown → auto-dismiss → attract state
8. Phone: receives session_ended → shows RecapView (already exists)
9. Backend: _auto_advance_queue() promotes next user → queue_updated fires
```

## Edge Cases

- **User speaking when limit hits**: `wrapping_up` guard in `handle_event()` ignores new events
- **No liked items**: Closing speech handles gracefully ("We explored a lot of styles"), recap hides "Your Favorites" section
- **Closing speech generation fails**: Hardcoded fallback message used
- **TTS still playing when recap appears**: Recap renders as overlay while TTS finishes — no conflict
- **Force-end/disconnect**: These skip the closing speech (already abrupt by design), but still emit `session_ended` so recap screen still shows if data is available

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` | Replace silent limit, add `_graceful_shutdown()`, `_generate_closing_speech()`, `wrapping_up` guard, `user_name` in payload |
| `frontend/src/components/mirror/SessionRecap.tsx` | **New file** — recap overlay component |
| `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx` | Add `"recap"` state, recap data state, update `session_ended` handler, auto-dismiss timer, render SessionRecap |
| `frontend/src/app/phone/page.tsx` | Fix `session_ended` payload field name mismatch |

## Verification

1. Start a session via admin dashboard
2. Send 20+ voice messages to hit the API limit
3. Verify: Mira speaks a closing recap via TTS
4. Verify: Mirror shows recap overlay with summary, liked items (if any), stats
5. Verify: Recap auto-dismisses after ~20 seconds → mirror returns to attract
6. Verify: Phone shows recap view with correct stats
7. Verify: Queue auto-advances to next user
8. Verify: Force-end from admin still works (skips closing speech, shows recap if data available)

"""Tests for _call_claude error recovery — orphaned tool_use cleanup.

When a Claude API call fails mid-conversation, the error handler must clean up
the conversation history so subsequent calls don't hit permanent 400 errors from
unmatched tool_use/tool_result pairs.
"""

from types import SimpleNamespace
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator, SessionState


def _make_orchestrator() -> MiraOrchestrator:
    """Create an orchestrator with mocked Socket.io."""
    orch = MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=AsyncMock())
    return orch


def _make_session(history: list) -> SessionState:
    """Create a session with pre-populated conversation history."""
    session = SessionState(user_id="test-user")
    session.conversation_history = list(history)
    session.system_prompt = "You are a test assistant."
    return session


def _make_tool_use_block(**kwargs):
    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK tool_use content block."""
    defaults = {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_123", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}
    defaults.update(kwargs)
    return SimpleNamespace(**defaults)


def _make_text_block(text="Hello"):
    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK text content block."""
    return SimpleNamespace(type="text", text=text)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_pops_user_message():
    """When the API fails, the last user message should be popped."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "What should I wear?"}
    session = _make_session([user_msg])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    assert session.conversation_history == []


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_pops_orphaned_tool_use_assistant():
    """When the API fails after a tool cycle, both the user tool_results AND
    the assistant tool_use message should be popped to prevent 400 errors."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()

    original_user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Find me a jacket"}
    assistant_with_tool_use = {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": [_make_tool_use_block()],
    }
    user_tool_results = {
        "role": "user",
        "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_123", "content": "{}"}],
    }
    session = _make_session([original_user_msg, assistant_with_tool_use, user_tool_results])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    # Only the original user message should remain
    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1
    assert session.conversation_history[0] is original_user_msg


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_preserves_text_only_assistant():
    """When the API fails, a text-only assistant message should NOT be popped —
    only assistant messages containing tool_use blocks are orphaned."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()

    user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}
    assistant_text_only = {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": [_make_text_block("Hey there!")],
    }
    user_msg2 = {"role": "user", "content": "What's in style?"}
    session = _make_session([user_msg, assistant_text_only, user_msg2])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    # user_msg2 popped, but assistant_text_only preserved (no tool_use blocks)
    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 2
    assert session.conversation_history[0] is user_msg
    assert session.conversation_history[1] is assistant_text_only


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fallback_message_streamed_on_failure():
    """When the API fails, a fallback voice message should be streamed
    so the user gets verbal feedback."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        with patch.object(orch, "_stream_text", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_stream:
            await orch._call_claude(session)

    # Should have been called twice: once with fallback text, once with end_of_message
    assert mock_stream.call_count == 2
    fallback_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[0]
    assert "glitched" in fallback_call.args[1]
    eom_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[1]
    assert eom_call.args[1] == ""
    assert eom_call.kwargs.get("end_of_message") is True


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_handles_dict_tool_use_blocks():
    """The orphaned tool_use check should work with plain dict blocks
    (not just Anthropic SDK objects), since content can be either format."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()

    original_user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Find me shoes"}
    assistant_with_dict_tool_use = {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": [{"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_456", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}],
    }
    user_tool_results = {
        "role": "user",
        "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_456", "content": "{}"}],
    }
    session = _make_session([original_user_msg, assistant_with_dict_tool_use, user_tool_results])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    # Both tool_results user msg and tool_use assistant msg should be popped
    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1
    assert session.conversation_history[0] is original_user_msg

async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
        """Handle an incoming event and generate Mira's response.

        Events are: voice, gesture (swipe_right, swipe_left, thumbs_up, thumbs_down),
        pose, snapshot, silence, session_start.
        """
        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
        if not session or not session.is_active:
            print(f"[mira] handle_event: no active session for {user_id}, ignoring event {event.get('type', '?')}")
            return

        # Block all new events once graceful shutdown has started
        if session.wrapping_up:
            print(f"[mira] Session wrapping up for {user_id}, ignoring event {event.get('type', '?')}")
            return

input.content:
# Plan: Fix Conversation History Corruption (Permanent 400 Errors)

## Context

Mira's orchestrator suffers from a **concurrency race condition** that permanently corrupts `conversation_history`. When two socket events (e.g., voice + gesture, or voice + silence timer) enter `handle_event()` concurrently, they interleave at `await` points during Claude streaming and tool execution. This inserts a regular user message between an assistant's `tool_use` block and its expected `tool_result`, violating Claude's API contract. Once corrupted, **every subsequent Claude call fails with a 400 error** for the rest of the session, and the user hears "my brain glitched" repeatedly.

The error from the logs:
```
messages.2: `tool_use` ids were found without `tool_result` blocks immediately after: REDACTED
```

### Root Cause

`handle_event()` has no concurrency guard. The `is_processing` flag is set but **never checked at entry** — only the silence timer respects it. Two async events can both enter, both append user messages, and corrupt the history.

### Why Current Error Recovery Fails

The `except` block in `_call_claude()` (line 321-344) pops only the **last 1-2 messages**. But corruption can happen deeper in the history (e.g., at `messages[2]`), and the shallow cleanup doesn't reach it. Once the corrupted pair persists, every future call sends the same broken history.

## Changes

### 1. `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` — Add event serialization lock

Add an `asyncio.Lock` per session to serialize all event processing:

```python
# In SessionState dataclass:
_event_lock: asyncio.Lock = field(default_factory=asyncio.Lock)
```

In `handle_event()`, acquire the lock before any history modifications:
```python
async with session._event_lock:
    session.is_processing = True
    try:
        # ... existing event processing ...
    finally:
        session.is_processing = False
```

**Why a lock instead of just checking `is_processing`**: A flag check has a TOCTOU window in theory (though safe in single-threaded asyncio). More importantly, the lock **queues** events rather than dropping them — if the user speaks while Claude is processing a tool chain, their transcript waits and processes after, rather than being silently discarded.

### 2. `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` — Add history validation before Claude calls

Add `_validate_history()` method that runs at the top of `_call_claude()`. This is **defense-in-depth** — even with the lock, edge cases (bugs, future changes) could corrupt history. The validator:

1. Ensures alternating `user` → `assistant` → `user` roles
2. Ensures every assistant message with `tool_use` blocks is followed by a user message with matching `tool_result` blocks
3. If corruption is detected, **truncates history** back to the last valid point rather than attempting surgical repair (simpler, more reliable)
4. Logs a warning when repair occurs

### 3. `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` — Fix missing `end_of_message` in outer catch

The `handle_event()` outer `except` (line 247-249) streams the fallback text but never sends `end_of_message=True`. The frontend accumulates the text in `responseAccumulatorRef` but never flushes it — Mira appears to freeze silently. Add the missing signal.

### 4. `backend/tests/test_history_integrity.py` — New comprehensive test file

Test the lock, validation, and recovery across these scenarios:

| Test | What it covers |
|------|----------------|
| `test_concurrent_events_serialized` | Two events entering `handle_event` simultaneously are serialized by the lock — history stays valid |
| `test_event_during_tool_execution` | Event arrives while tools are executing (between tool_use append and tool_result append) — lock prevents interleaving |
| `test_validate_history_orphaned_tool_use` | History with tool_use but no tool_result is truncated to last valid point |
| `test_validate_history_consecutive_user_messages` | Two consecutive user messages are truncated |
| `test_validate_history_clean_passthrough` | Valid history passes through unchanged |
| `test_validate_history_deep_corruption` | Corruption at messages[2] (matching the real error) is repaired |
| `test_is_processing_prevents_silence_during_lock` | Silence timer correctly skips when processing is locked |
| `test_fallback_sends_end_of_message` | Outer catch sends both fallback text AND end_of_message signal |
| `test_error_recovery_after_tool_chain` | API failure during depth-2 tool recursion recovers cleanly |

Testing approach follows existing `test_error_recovery.py` patterns: `_make_orchestrator()` with `AsyncMock` sio, `_make_session()` with pre-built history, `patch.object` on `orch.client.messages.stream`.

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` | Add `_event_lock` to SessionState, wrap `handle_event` body with `async with`, add `_validate_history()`, fix outer catch missing `end_of_message` |
| `backend/tests/test_history_integrity.py` | **New file** — 9 tests covering concurrency, validation, and recovery |

## Verification

```bash
cd backend
pytest tests/test_history_integrity.py -v    # New tests
pytest tests/test_error_recovery.py -v        # Existing tests still pass
pytest tests/ -x -q                           # Full suite
```

-8. Verify: Force-end from admin still works (skips closing speech, shows recap if data available)
